<!-- GENERATED by sv-playbook constitution set — do not edit by hand -->

# Principles

- **Machine-first: anything a machine can do, a machine must do - never an agent over chat, never a human. An LLM or a person doing derivable, executable work (state reconciliation, merges, bookkeeping, status relay) is a defect, not a workflow.**: Founder ruling 2026-07-11 (verbatim: 'todo lo que pueda hacer una maquina lo tiene que hacer, no puede hacerlo un agente por chat o un humano'). Measured cost of violating it: one night of PM chat-work walking merge cascades, closing merged packets and refreshing backups by hand - all derivable diffs plus executable commands. Chat agents are for judgment (product, priorities, review semantics); machines are for facts and convergence (FLOW-010 reconciler, MERGE-QUEUE, DISPATCH-PLAN).
- **Semantic kernel: agents receive only the semantic residue of any workflow. Before delegating to an agent, extract every deterministic sub-step into code (gates, scripts, preflights); the agent gets the mechanical results as input and contributes judgment only. Delegating derivable work to an agent is a design defect.**: Founder ruling 2026-07-11 (verbatim: 'tenemos que delegar lo menos posible a los agentes. lo que es determinista y podemos averiguarlo nosotros por codigo, lo hacemos. los agentes se encargan de la parte semantica'). Complements machine-first (cf11bd51): that one says who does derivable work; this one is the design razor for building every feature. First application: review preflight - tonight's reviewers spent most of their tokens on deterministic checks (sha match, clean verify, write_set diff, RED-test-name grep, CI status) that a script does for free.
- **Founder-led operating chain is Founder -> Founder Interface -> Orchestrator/TL -> Implementers and Reviewers. The Founder Interface self-rehydrates principles, constitution, decisions, role context and live state; it never asks the founder to repeat prior decisions, implements code, or reviews implementation.**: Provider conversations are disposable. The playbook is the provider-agnostic source of truth, and repeated founder corrections must become durable rails instead of chat memory.
- **A Delivery Orchestrator is a bounded operational role: it dispatches and verifies but never edits implementation or reviewer worktrees, writes production code, or reviews its own dispatch. Every session proves role bootstrap before acting; missing capability enforcement is a system rail defect.**: Repeated human or founder-interface corrections are not a control mechanism. Role knowledge and capability boundaries must travel through the provider adapter and be mechanically checked before dispatch.
- **Binding decisions are proactive context, not a searchable archive. Every agent session receives a verified role-scoped bootstrap bundle before acting; dispatch refuses without current bootstrap evidence.**: A founder should not repeat a decision because an agent failed to discover it. Persistence prevents loss; mandatory role-scoped delivery prevents omission.
